description.p: NetGrip shows your machine's network interfaces as boxes on a canvas: every IP configuration, VLAN and bond is its own box, joined to its interface by a line. Drag an IP configuration to another NIC to move it, drag two NICs together to create a bond, right-click to add VLANs or change bonding modes.
It manages the local machine or any remote Linux host reachable over SSH, and always shows the exact iproute2 commands it will run before applying anything.

Docker visibility, read-only. A docker bridge is labelled by its network name, and each running container is drawn as a single box on the networks it joins, showing its image, its IP per network and its compose project/service; the container's host-side veth is folded into that box. Published ports draw as dashed connectors to the host address they bind to, and each container's outbound default route as a dotted line to the uplink, both keyed in the legend and toggleable from the View menu. Read best-effort via docker inspect, so a host without docker is unaffected; docker-owned links are read-only.

Persistence and clarity. Changes can now Save across reboots through the host's own backend (netplan, systemd-networkd, NetworkManager or ifupdown), with Try (apply then auto-revert) and Apply alongside it. Addresses are grouped per protocol into IPv4/IPv6 boxes carrying their gateway and DNS; a System DNS box shows where each resolver comes from. Adds a View menu, a legend overlay, wired/wireless glyphs, veth pairing, vlan-aware bridge tags, draft VLANs, Windows as an SSH client, and one-command installers for Linux and Windows.
Initial release: canvas view, IP config move/clone by drag, bonds and VLANs, SSH remote hosts, demo mode.
